code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test31-JUL-2015Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, mild hyperchloremia, low CO₂, and significant proteinuria and glucosuria with low urine specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal). These findings suggest impaired renal function, possibly chronic kidney disease with tubular dysfunction (suggested by glucosuria with normal blood glucose). No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend nephrology evaluation and further renal workup.
